decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.28°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective and conservative first step is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader material to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hotspot.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity by 1.5x (within allowed 2.0x limit) enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized temperature gradient under the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if conductivity becomes too high relative to the base plate extraction capacity
If this single change is insufficient, subsequent steps may require increasing spreader geometry or base plate conductivity
